title: ED discharge documentation
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports documenting the clinician-confirmed diagnosis or outcome and plan, patient understanding, specifically required follow-up and safety-net communication.
assessment.rationale: The exact patient-communication section supports documenting patient concerns or goals, the clinician-confirmed plan, completed risk discussion and specifically arranged follow-up; it does not decide discharge or generate advice.

source.title: Discharge to General Practice
source.jurisdiction: United Kingdom emergency and primary care; requires UAE pathway adaptation
source.population: Adult and pediatric patients being discharged directly from an emergency department.
source.setting: Emergency-department discharge communication with patients and primary care.
source.exclusions: Exact for recording the clinician-confirmed outcome, treatment plan, information communicated, required follow-up, responsible party, discharge destination and safety-net discussion. It does not make the discharge decision or generate advice.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting the clinician-confirmed diagnosis or outcome and plan, patient understanding, specifically required follow-up and safety-net communication.

assessment.text: Supports recording the presenting complaint, investigations or procedures, outcome, follow-up, responsible party, referral and discharge destination in a discharge handover.
assessment.rationale: The exact discharge-letter section supports recording measured triage data, investigations or procedures already performed, disposition, referral and handover only as clinician-confirmed facts.
